commands.label: Refresh store metadata packet
commands.purpose: Verify listing metadata, screenshots, privacy declarations, and copy-risk checks before account work.

commands.label: Bind Expo project
commands.purpose: Create the EAS project binding on the target Expo account and store only the generated key name location in app config.

commands.label: Prepare credential templates
commands.purpose: Generate the key-name setup packet, empty environment template, and EAS binding template without secret values.

commands.label: Check credential keys
commands.purpose: Verify Expo, App Store Connect, and Google Play key names without printing values.

commands.label: Validate native QA evidence
commands.purpose: Verify Android and iOS physical-device evidence before store submission.

commands.label: Run strict EAS gate
commands.purpose: Block TestFlight, Play internal testing, and production submission until account-bound prerequisites are ready.

commands.label: Submit iOS build
commands.purpose: Submit the production iOS build after strict EAS and native QA evidence pass.

commands.label: Submit Android build
commands.purpose: Submit the production Android build after strict EAS and native QA evidence pass.

phases.action: Regenerate and review the store submission packet.
phases.label: Store metadata readiness

phases.action: Run EAS init on the target Expo account, then commit only the app-config binding location.
phases.label: EAS project binding

phases.action: Set EXPO_TOKEN in CI or the local release shell.
phases.label: Expo automation token

phases.action: Set the App Store Connect app id plus Apple ID or API key environment names outside the repository.
phases.label: iOS submit account

phases.action: Set one Google Play service-account credential key in CI or the local release shell.
phases.label: Android submit account

phases.action: Fill native QA evidence with real Android and iOS physical-device runs, then validate it.
phases.label: Native QA evidence

phases.action: Clear metadata, EAS binding, Expo token, and store account prerequisites before running strict EAS.
evidence: terminal output from npm run release:eas:strict
phases.label: Strict EAS gate

phases.action: Submit only after metadata, native QA evidence, and strict EAS are verified.
phases.label: Store submit

summary.nextAction: Run EAS init on the target Expo account, then commit only the app-config binding location.
